predicate_note: Edge direction: Disease (subject) → has_phenotype → PhenotypicFeature (object). PloverDB stores 198 disease→HP:0001250 edges, but the answer set is heavily skewed toward oncology (see kg_coverage_note below).

validation.predicate_fix: REPLACED: Original Q6 used HP:0004401 (meconium ileus) which normalizes to MONDO:0054868 (Disease type). New Q6 uses HP:0001250 (seizures).

verification_guide.summary: 2 brain tumors that frequently present with seizures, each supported by a peer-reviewed neurology / neuro-oncology publication that gives a specific seizure-presentation rate. Both are in PloverDB's returned set for this query.
sources_used: Duehrsen L et al. 2019. "Seizures as presenting symptom in patients with glioblastoma." Epilepsia 60(1):149-154. PMID:30536819. Europe PMC: https://europepmc.org/article/MED/30536819
Kerkhof M, Vecht CJ. 2013. "Seizure characteristics and prognostic factors of gliomas." Epilepsia 54 Suppl 9:12-17. PMID:24328866. Europe PMC: https://europepmc.org/article/MED/24328866
verification_guide.how_to_verify_an_entry: Open the Europe PMC URL for the cited paper. The page renders the paper's title, authors, journal/year/volume, PMID, and full abstract in a single "Abstract" block near the top. The exact_quote field reproduces one or two sentences from that abstract that give the seizure-presentation statistic for the cited disease. Europe PMC abstracts do not reflow — locate the quoted sentence by scanning the abstract paragraph for the cited percentage or rate.
verification_guide.cross_check_with_ploverdb: Both verified_answer CURIEs (MONDO:0018177 glioblastoma, MONDO:0016696 anaplastic oligodendroglioma) were confirmed present in the PloverDB result set for the gold one-hop query (n0=biolink:Disease, n1=HP:0001250, e0=biolink:has_phenotype). PloverDB returned 198 disease CURIEs total.
verification_guide.kg_coverage_note: PloverDB's 198 disease results for HP:0001250 (Seizure) are ~190 brain tumors + a small handful of other entries (Cowden disease, diffuse meningeal melanocytosis). Classical epilepsy syndromes that clinicians strongly associate with seizures — Dravet syndrome, Lennox-Gastaut syndrome, tuberous sclerosis complex, Rett syndrome, Angelman syndrome, Smith-Lemli-Opitz syndrome, infantile spasms, febrile seizures, etc. — are NOT in PloverDB's answer set for this query. This is a KG coverage gap: KG2c's disease→phenotype edges with HP:0001250 appear to come predominantly from oncology / clinical-trial annotation sources, not from HPO disease annotation databases or epilepsy classification references. The gold set is restricted to what is actually returnable; this asymmetry should be discussed in the paper's Limitations section. See also pipeline/benchmark/known_issues.md for related canonicalization quirks affecting this question.
verification_guide.predicate_alternative_explored: We also confirmed that swapping the predicate to biolink:causes (as q3 does for warfarin AEs) returns a different set of diseases. The has_phenotype edge in this KG build models "this disease has Seizure as a clinical feature"; results bias toward conditions whose phenotype profiles were imported from oncology trial annotations rather than HPO disease records.

verified_answers.label: glioblastoma
verified_answers.presentation_rate: 22.3% of patients (Duehrsen 2019 cohort, n=103); 30-60% across larger series (Kerkhof & Vecht 2013 review)

evidence.page_title: Seizures as presenting symptom in patients with glioblastoma. — Europe PMC
evidence.publisher: European Bioinformatics Institute — Europe PMC (mirror of PubMed)
evidence.citation: Dührsen L, Sauvigny T, Ricklefs FL, Mende KC, Schaper M, Matschke J, Goebell E, Westphal M, Martens T. Seizures as presenting symptom in patients with glioblastoma. Epilepsia. 2019;60(1):149-154. PMID:30536819.
evidence.where_on_page: Open the URL. Near the top of the page, after the title "Seizures as presenting symptom in patients with glioblastoma." and the author/journal line, scroll to the "Abstract" block. The abstract is a single paragraph organized as Objective / Methods / Results / Conclusion. Under "Results" (about a third of the way into the paragraph) you'll find the sentence beginning "One hundred three patients diagnosed with glioblastoma..." — the very next sentence is the cited statistic.
evidence.exact_quote: Twenty-three patients were symptomatic with seizures (22.3%).

verified_answers.label: anaplastic oligodendroglioma
verified_answers.presentation_rate: 60-85% (low-grade gliomas, of which oligodendroglioma is the canonical example; Kerkhof & Vecht 2013 review)

evidence.page_title: Seizure characteristics and prognostic factors of gliomas. — Europe PMC
evidence.publisher: European Bioinformatics Institute — Europe PMC (mirror of PubMed)
evidence.citation: Kerkhof M, Vecht CJ. Seizure characteristics and prognostic factors of gliomas. Epilepsia. 2013;54 Suppl 9:12-17. PMID:24328866. (Review article.)
evidence.where_on_page: Open the URL. After the title "Seizure characteristics and prognostic factors of gliomas." and the author/journal line (Epilepsia, 2013), the "Abstract" block sits near the top. The abstract opens with the sentence "Epilepsy in neuroepithelial tumors is highly prevalent." — the immediately following sentence is the cited statistic giving seizure-incidence ranges for each tumor type, including oligodendroglioma's category (low-grade gliomas).
evidence.exact_quote: Neurogliomas (dysembryoplastic neuroepitheliomas [DNETs] and gangliogliomas) have a seizure incidence of 80-100%, low-grade gliomas of 60-85%, and glioblastoma of 30-60%. With each type, the appearance of seizures is usually the presenting clinical symptom.
